subject: re: Doctor Victor & Sterling Bank
body:
I spoke with both Steve Orenstein from Sterling Bank and Doctor Victor this
afternoon.  Steve informed me of the following:

 

a)      Currently Doctor Victor has a credit line in the amount of $500,000.
This has been in place for about 4-5 months and has the maximum amount of
roughly $460,000 is  outstanding.  Doctor Victor gave a personal and
corporate guarantee in addition to an all asset lien.

b)      Equipment financing has been done with Sterling Bank for 4-5 pieces
of equipment.  These loans are amortized over 60 months and the outstanding
balance is just about $400,000.

c)       Factoring of receivables has been discussed however he stated
Sterling is not competitive in that arena.  He said Doctor Victor has 2
receivables outstanding from Saks and Shop NBC estimated at $500,000.  He
said they would advance between 75-80% of that total and then collect from
vendors directly.  If not collected in 30/60/90 days then interest would be
charged back to Doctor Victor.

 

I tried to obtain more information from the banker  on Factoring however he
stated that was not his specialty and that he would have to talk to the
Factoring Department.  He told me that his understanding from Doctor Victor
was that we were looking to help by co signing on his line of credit and
extending it from $500,000 to $1,000,000.  I told him that I was not aware
of that and that I was to told to obtain facts on factoring.  Doctor Victor
then called and told me that he does not need JEE help for factoring and was
under the impression that you were going to help by cosigning on line of
credit.  I told him that I would communicate with you and we would speak
next week.  Doctor Victor said he may try to call you on your cell phone
this weekend.

 

 

 

Richard Kahn

917-414-7584
body_html:
I spoke with both Steve Orenstein from Sterling Bank and Doctor Victor this afternoon.  Steve informed me of the following:

 

a)      Currently Doctor Victor has a credit line in the amount of $500,000.  This has been in place for about 4-5 months and has the maximum amount of roughly $460,000 is  outstanding.  Doctor Victor gave a personal and  corporate guarantee in addition to an all asset lien.

b)      Equipment financing has been done with Sterling Bank for 4-5 pieces of equipment.  These loans are amortized over 60 months and the outstanding balance is just about $400,000.

c)       Factoring of receivables has been discussed however he stated Sterling is not competitive in that arena.  He said Doctor Victor has 2 receivables outstanding from Saks and Shop NBC estimated at $500,000.  He said they would advance between 75-80% of that total and then collect from vendors directly.  If not collected in 30/60/90 days then interest would be charged back to Doctor Victor.

 

I tried to obtain more information from the banker  on Factoring however he stated that was not his specialty and that he would have to talk to the Factoring Department.  He told me that his understanding from Doctor Victor was that we were looking to help by co signing on his line of credit and extending it from $500,000 to $1,000,000.  I told him that I was not aware of that and that I was to told to obtain facts on factoring.  Doctor Victor then called and told me that he does not need JEE help for factoring and was under the impression that you were going to help by cosigning on line of credit.  I told him that I would communicate with you and we would speak next week.  Doctor Victor said he may try to call you on your cell phone this weekend.

 

 

 

Richard Kahn

917-414-7584
